skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI video prompts and run inference.sh belt CLI examples for text-to-video, image-to-video, avatars, lipsync, upscaling, foley, and video merging.
skill.summary: Run AI video generation workflows through the inference.sh belt CLI.
skill.author: infsh-skills

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command and URL patterns plus two weak cryptography alerts. Review confirms the command patterns are legitimate belt and npx examples, but they create real third-party network and account-credit exposure. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives from descriptive text, and no prompt injection or malicious exfiltration intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Networked CLI Execution Sends User Content to Third-Party Services
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash use for belt and repeatedly instructs users to run belt app commands with prompts, image URLs, audio URLs, and video URLs. This is expected for AI video generation, but it can transmit user-provided media and text to inference.sh hosted models and may consume account credits.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly grants Bash access to belt and shows concrete belt app run commands with remote media URLs. The behavior is legitimate for the skill purpose, but the data and billing exposure are real.

medium_findings.title: Remote Skill Installation Examples Introduce Supply Chain Risk
medium_findings.description: The related skills section gives npx skills add commands that install additional skills from a remote repository. These commands are documentation examples rather than allowed tool permissions, but following them expands trusted code and should be reviewed before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npx install commands are directly present and target remote skill packages. Risk is moderated because they are examples and the skill metadata only allows Bash(belt *).

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography at the frontmatter description and a table header. No cryptographic API, hash function, encryption routine, key material, or authentication logic appears at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of both lines shows descriptive marketplace text and a Markdown table header only. There is no code path that performs cryptography.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Placeholder Media References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to the inference.sh site, an image asset, install instructions, documentation links, and placeholder media URLs. They are not evidence of covert exfiltration, but users should understand that real media URLs sent to belt are processed remotely.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links and example inputs aligned with the skill purpose. No hidden endpoint, credential transfer, or obfuscation was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed Bash Command Wrapper for belt CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill declares Bash(belt *) and provides many belt app run examples. This enables external command execution against a networked CLI and requires user awareness of data transfer and billing effects.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The permission declaration and examples are explicit. The pattern is expected but still security-relevant because it executes an external networked tool.

content.user_title: Generate AI Videos With CLI Models
content.value_statement: AI video workflows require precise model choices and valid inputs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through inference.sh belt commands for video generation tasks.
actual_capabilities: Lists supported text-to-video, image-to-video, avatar, lipsync, foley, upscaling, and merge models.
Provides belt CLI commands for running video generation apps through inference.sh.
Shows input patterns for prompts, image URLs, audio URLs, video URLs, durations, and transitions.
Helps choose model families such as Veo, Wan, Seedance, Grok, OmniHuman, Fabric, and PixVerse.
Links to inference.sh documentation for running apps and streaming results.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh belt CLI and an authenticated account.
Generated video quality, speed, and cost depend on the selected hosted model.
Input media must be available through URLs accepted by the remote service.
The skill does not edit local video files directly without external model execution.

use_cases.title: Create Social Media Clips
use_cases.description: Generate short videos from campaign prompts, product images, or visual concepts using hosted video models.

use_cases.title: Prototype Product Demo Footage
use_cases.description: Turn storyboard ideas into test clips before committing to filming or manual animation.

use_cases.title: Produce Avatar and Lipsync Assets
use_cases.description: Create talking-head clips from portrait images and narration audio for training or explainer content.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a short AI video of [scene]. Use a fast model and explain the belt command before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Animate an Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this image URL: [image URL]. Recommend a suitable image-to-video model and use natural motion.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Talking Avatar
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking avatar from [portrait URL] and [audio URL]. Choose the model and describe expected inputs.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Video Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a workflow that creates clips, adds foley, upscales the result, and merges scenes for [campaign goal].

output_examples.input: User asks for a five-second beach sunset video with waves and camera motion.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a text-to-video model, prepares the scene prompt, notes likely duration support, and runs the matching belt command.

output_examples.input: User provides a portrait image URL and a narration audio URL.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects an avatar or lipsync model, confirms the remote media inputs, and starts a talking-head generation job.

output_examples.input: User has two generated clips and wants a polished final asset.
output_examples.output: The assistant suggests foley, upscaling, and merge steps, then sequences the belt commands in production order.

best_practices: Confirm that prompts and media URLs are safe to send to third-party hosted models.
Choose the lowest-cost fast model for drafts before using premium quality models.
Keep source URLs, generated outputs, and model choices documented for repeatable production.
anti_patterns: Do not send private, regulated, or unreleased media unless the service terms allow it.
Do not run remote skill installation commands without reviewing the package source.
Do not assume every model supports the same duration, resolution, audio, or input fields.

faq.question: Does this skill generate video locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the inference.sh belt CLI to call hosted video generation apps.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it create videos from images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes image-to-video examples for models such as Wan and Seedance.

faq.question: Can it create lipsync or avatar videos?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for OmniHuman, Fabric, and PixVerse lipsync workflows.

faq.question: What should users check before running commands?
faq.answer: Users should confirm account login, model cost, input media permissions, and privacy requirements.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally runs a networked CLI and sends prompts or media URLs to hosted services.
